title: P0 integrity: ADR-0072 system-failure HALT+diagnose is UNWIRED in fleet mode — verdict-incoherence sets the flag + logs 'halting' but the fleet path never halts, escalates, or files the P0 (the 862→899 livelock re-opens under the default fleet.count>1)
root_cause: ADR-0072's verdict-coherence floor (d531fc32) was validated on the SINGLE-CYCLE path only. cyclerun.go:228-233 (orchestrator, per cycle) DETECTS the incoherence (recorded FAIL vs on-disk audit=PASS + acs=PASS), sets result.SystemFailure, and PRINTS '[orchestrator] SYSTEM-FAILURE HALT … Halting the loop for pipeline diagnosis (ADR-0072)' — but it does NOT itself halt or escalate; the comment explicitly delegates the halt to 'the batch loop'. The actual halt+diagnose lives in cmd_loop.go:650 (`if result.SystemFailure.Halt`): writePipelineEscalation → .evolve/pipeline-escalation.json + file a P0 pipeline-repair inbox item + StopReason='system_failure_halt' + return 4. BUT the fleet/swarm dispatch path NEVER inspects result.SystemFailure (repo-wide grep across internal/fleet, internal/swarm, internal/phases/swarmrunner, internal/fanoutdispatch = ZERO references). So under the default fleet mode (policy.json fleet.count=2, swarm stage=shadow), each lane sets the flag + logs the promissory 'HALT' line and then ADVANCES to the next cycle — no escalation dossier (verified .evolve/pipeline-escalation.json ABSENT after 2 halts this session), no fresh P0 filed, no rc=4. The loop churns on the forged verdicts exactly as in the 862→899 livelock ADR-0072 was built to break. Live evidence 2026-07-18: loop b2ff7gjfl cycles 921/923 both logged the halt, both continued, neither produced the artifacts.
evidence: go/internal/core/cyclerun.go:228-233 (detect + set + LOG only, delegates to batch loop); go/cmd/evolve/cmd_loop.go:650-658 (the real halt+escalate+P0+rc4, SINGLE-CYCLE path); grep result.SystemFailure in internal/fleet|swarm|swarmrunner|fanoutdispatch = empty (fleet never consumes it); .evolve/policy.json fleet.count=2; runtime: .evolve/pipeline-escalation.json absent + no pipeline-repair inbox item after this session's 2 verdict-incoherence halts; loop log b2ff7gjfl (cycles 921/923).
fix: Make the fleet/swarm result aggregation honor ADR-0072: on any lane whose result.SystemFailure.Halt is true, (1) write the pipeline-escalation dossier, (2) file the P0 pipeline-repair inbox item, (3) HALT the batch (stop dispatching new cycles/lanes; propagate rc=4). CENTRALIZE the halt+escalate+P0 logic (currently inline in cmd_loop.go:650) into a shared function both the single-cycle and fleet paths call — never-duplicate, and so the two enforcement surfaces cannot drift again (this drift is exactly what let the fleet path skip the floor). Decide the multi-lane semantics explicitly: a system-failure in ANY lane should stop the batch (a forged verdict means the pipeline is untrustworthy for ALL lanes), draining in-flight lanes gracefully. Add a fleet-path regression test: a lane returning SystemFailure.Halt must produce the escalation + P0 + batch halt (RED first — today it does none).
acceptance: Fleet/swarm regression test: a lane result with SystemFailure.Halt=true triggers escalation dossier + P0 pipeline-repair inbox item + batch halt (rc=4). Fails today (RED).
The halt+escalate+P0 logic is a single shared function called by BOTH cmd_loop.go (single-cycle) and the fleet path — no duplicated logic.
Live: a verdict-incoherence in fleet mode writes .evolve/pipeline-escalation.json AND files a P0, AND stops the batch — no more churn-on-forged-verdict.
cd go && go test -race on touched pkgs + acs-durable green; the ADR-0072 single-cycle behavior is unchanged (existing tests still pass).
source: operator question 2026-07-18 ('why it halt instead of trigger diagnosis') exposed that ADR-0072's halt+diagnose is single-cycle-only; the default fleet mode logs the halt but never acts on it. Note: the verdict-incoherence itself was aggravated this session by a concurrent -race -tags integration test run flaking the integration tier (TestFleetSoak) into a false FAIL — see integration_tier_scope_flake.
